title: Seismicity in the upper plate of the Northern Chilean offshore forearc: Evidence of splay fault south of the Mejillones Peninsula
abstract: A 3D tomographic model of the upper plate was developed using arrival times from the 1995 CINCA seismic network aftershock catalog from the Antofagasta earthquake (Mw 8.1). Seismicity partially defined by a clear lineament between 22 and 5 km depth is interpreted as an active splay fault ~50 km long south of the Mejillones Peninsula. The splay fault zone shows decreased Vp and Vs velocities and increased Vp/Vs ratio suggesting fracturing and fluid saturation.

location.description: Northern Chilean offshore forearc south of Mejillones Peninsula (~23°S); splay fault imaging using CINCA seismic network

data.name: CINCA95 Temporary Seismic Network — Antofagasta Aftershock Catalog (~23°S)
data.description: 35 three-component seismometers (1 Hz MARK L4-3D, 100 Hz sampling) deployed onshore/offshore Antofagasta, Aug–Oct 1995. Waveform data freely accessible on GEOFON website.

data.name: IPOC Permanent Network — Northern Chile
data.description: Integrated Plate Boundary Observatory Chile (IPOC) permanent network (code CX), 30 stations at -24.6° to -17.6°S. miniSEED waveforms freely accessible on GEOFON.

data.name: MEJIPE Temporary Network — Mejillones Peninsula 2013–2015
data.description: MEJIPE experiment instruments provided by GIPP of GFZ Potsdam (Salazar et al., 2013). Data currently restricted and not available to the scientific community.

data.name: CSN Seismic Catalog — Centro Sismológico Nacional
data.description: CSN permanent network catalog (36,456 events) used for 3D tomography model; freely accessible at www.sismología.cl.

_data_note: CINCA and IPOC network data freely available on GEOFON. CSN catalog freely available on sismologia.cl. MEJIPE network data restricted (GFZ). TaskForce network also used — data availability unclear.

processing.summary: New catalog of 1995 Antofagasta earthquake (Mw 8.1) aftershocks was built using the CINCA temporary network via the BackTrackBB automated detection algorithm. Events were manually relocated using SEISAN and NonLinLoc. A 3D Vp and Vp/Vs tomography model was computed using the INSIGHT code (double-difference tomography), combining CINCA, TaskForce, MEJIPE, IPOC and CSN catalogs. Focal mechanisms determined using FOCMEC. Splay fault identified in the outer wedge between 5–22 km depth, ~50 km long south of Mejillones Peninsula.
workflow: 1. Automated event detection using BackTrackBB (kurtosis-based, eight 1–10 Hz frequency bands)
2. Manual P- and S-wave arrival time picking using SEISAN
3. Initial hypocentre relocation using NonLinLoc and 1D velocity model (Husen et al. 1999)
4. 3D tomography inversion for Vp and Vp/Vs using INSIGHT code (Potin 2016)
5. Final relocation using derived 3D velocity model
7. Repeating earthquake identification by waveform cross-correlation (Butterworth 1–10 Hz)
processing.notes: Passive network study only — no active source seismic. Multiple catalogs combined: CINCA (2212 events), TaskForce (586 events), MEJIPE (4604 events), CSN+IPOC (36,456 events).

analysis_notes: PDF confirmed. Major corrections applied: authors were completely wrong (Reginato et al. → Pastén-Araya et al. 10 authors); DOI corrected (228748 → 228706); vessel removed (no vessel — land/offshore passive network); channel_count added (35); sample_rate_ms added (10 ms = 100 Hz); data entries revised — CINCA/IPOC open on GEOFON, MEJIPE restricted (GFZ), CSN open at sismologia.cl; software corrected (BackTrackBB, SEISAN, NonLinLoc, INSIGHT, FOCMEC, SAC, GMT); workflow expanded from PDF methods; associated earthquakes corrected.
